title: vriesea subandina
intro: Vriesea subandina, also known as the Sub-Andean Air Plant, is a type of epiphytic bromeliad native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Vriesea subandina include the potential treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties, as well as the relief of digestive issues and wounds. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in folk remedies for its alleged ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
